title: Climate policy and environmental harm exposure
question: What financial risks arise from stricter climate policy or penalties for environmental harm?
guidance: Map exposure to energy, transport, and material costs, track upcoming regulations, and use basic carbon accounting to spot emission hotspots before they become cost or compliance problems.

title: Water scarcity exposure
question: How does water scarcity affect our operations?
guidance: Check whether your sites or suppliers sit in water-stressed regions, introduce water-saving practices, and prioritise suppliers with responsible water management.

title: Biodiversity regulation exposure
question: What constraints do biodiversity regulations create?
guidance: Screen site and supplier locations for biodiversity sensitivity, choose certified responsible sources, and document nature-related constraints before scaling.

title: Resource scarcity exposure
question: How does resource scarcity affect our business model?
guidance: Map critical materials, check supply stability and recyclability, and use circular design principles to reduce exposure to scarce inputs and waste-heavy operations.

title: Community and site stability exposure
question: What social risks affect our site stability?
guidance: Engage with local stakeholders early, keep feedback channels open, and make local value creation visible to reduce social friction around the site or operating model.

title: Consumer protection and governance exposure
question: What is the probability that evolving consumer protection and AI/advertising regulations will trigger complaints, fines, or loss of market access for our product?
guidance: Assign ownership for consumer protection and governance basics, simplify claims and pricing, review privacy and consent practices, and track complaints or false-claim signals.
